subject: First Year Algebra
instructions: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences.

problems.text: Suppose \(G\) is a group and \(H, K\) are normal subgroups of \(G\) such that \(H \cap K = 1\).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Show that there is an injective homomorphism \(G \to G/H \times G/K\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that if \(G = HK\) then \(G\) is isomorphic to \(G/H \times G/K\).

problems.text: Suppose \(G\) is a finite group, \(p\) is a prime, \(|G|\) is a power of \(p\) and \(G \times S \to S\) is an action of \(G\) on \(S\). Show that if \(p\) does not divide \(|S|\) then \(S\) has an element fixed by every element of \(G\).
Suppose \(p\) and \(q\) are primes such that \(p < q\) and \(p \mid q-1\). Show that there is a unique nonabelian group of order \(pq\), and show it is a semidirect product of two cyclic groups.

subparts.label: (iii)
subparts.text: Show that \(A_4\) is solvable but not nilpotent.

problems.text: Show that if \(n \geq 5\) then \(A_n\) has no proper subgroup of index less than \(n\) (you may assume results about the simplicity of \(A_n\)).
In both cases, justify your answer.

subparts.text: Find all conjugacy classes of elements of order \(4\) in \(S_6\).
